name: Cajun Prime Rib - Blackened
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the seasoning mix (optional):
- 1/4 cup Plus 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 cup Plus 2 teaspoons white pepper
- 1/4 cup Plus 2 teaspoons fennel seeds
- 1/4 cup Plus 3/4 teaspoon black pepper
- 2 1/2 teaspoons dry mustard
- 2 1/2 teaspoons ground cayenne pepper
2. Remove the fat cap from the top of the meat (butcher can do this for you) and save it.
3. Place the roast, standing on the rib bones, in a very large roasting pan.
4. Using a knife, make several punctures through the silver skin so seasoning can permeate the meat.
5. Pour a generous, even layer of black pepper over the top of the meat (the pepper should completely cover it).
6. Repeat with the garlic powder, then the salt, totally covering the preceding layer.
7. Carefully arrange the onions in an even layer on top so as not to knock off the seasoning.
8. Place the fat cap back on top and refrigerate for 24 hours.
9. Bake the ribs in a 550°F oven until the fat is dark brown and crispy, about 35 minutes.
10. Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
11. Refrigerate until well chilled, about 3 hours (this solidifies the juices and allows the steaks to be cooked rare).
12. Remove the fat cap and discard it.
13. Using a large knife blade, scrape off the onions and as much of the seasonings as possible and discard.
14. Slice between ribs into 6 steaks (4 will have bones); trim the cooked surface of meat from the 2 pieces that were on the outside of the roast.
15. Season and cook the steaks in your favorite way.
16. To blacken the steaks:
- Combine the seasoning mix ingredients thoroughly in a small bowl; you will have about 8 tablespoons.
- Sprinkle generously and evenly on both sides with the mix, using about 4 teaspoons per steak and pressing it in with hands.
17. Heat a cast iron skillet over very high heat until beyond the smoking stage and white ash forms on the skillet bottom—about 10 minutes.
18. Place one steak in the hot skillet (cook only one side at a time) and cook over very high heat until the underside develops a heavy, black crust, about 2 to 3 minutes.
19. Turn the steak over and cook until the underside is crusted like the first, about 2 to 3 more minutes.
20. Repeat with the remaining steaks.
21. Serve each steak while piping hot.
